meta.title: MB3R Lab | Sheaft resilience intelligence for networked systems
meta.description: Sheaft turns telemetry, topology and domain constraints into live attributed graphs for resilience assessment of networked systems.
meta.ogTitle: MB3R Lab | Sheaft resilience intelligence for networked systems
meta.ogDescription: Resilience intelligence for software infrastructure, smart mobility, and scientific reaction or interaction networks.
meta.twitterTitle: MB3R Lab | Sheaft resilience intelligence for networked systems
meta.twitterDescription: Model-based resilience intelligence for graph-structured networked systems.
meta.ogImageAlt: MB3R Lab networked systems resilience workflow
meta.twitterImageAlt: MB3R Lab networked systems resilience workflow

hero.title: Resilience intelligence for networked systems
hero.lead: Sheaft turns telemetry, topology and domain constraints into a live attributed graph, stress-tests disruption scenarios, and shows which critical operations are fragile before failures propagate.
hero.note: Built for graph-structured systems: software infrastructure, smart mobility, and scientific reaction or interaction networks.

panel.logicText: Components interact. Dependencies shift. Signals go missing. Failures propagate.
panel.graphText: Sheaft models the system as nodes, edges, constraints, telemetry, stressors and critical operations.

abstraction.title: One resilience model for graph-structured systems
abstraction.intro: Sheaft represents a system as an attributed graph: nodes, edges, constraints, telemetry, stressors and critical operations. The domain changes; the resilience question stays the same: what breaks, how far it propagates, and which operations are affected.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.text: Services, road segments, toll gates, sensors, proteins, metabolites, reactants.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.text: Latency, capacity, speed, confidence, energy, rate, state.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.text: Domain contracts, signal rules, conservation laws, biological priors.

stressors.title: Stressors
stressors.text: Crashes, demand surges, missing observations, perturbations.

verdict.title: Verdict
verdict.text: Fragile paths, blast radius, affected operations, resilience posture.

how.title: How Sheaft works
how.intro: Bering creates explicit graph artifacts. Sheaft evaluates disruption scenarios and produces resilience verdicts for critical operations.

discover.title: Discover the system graph
discover.text: Bering ingests telemetry, topology files, traces, event streams or explicit graph descriptions and builds a typed system model.

contracts.title: Attach domain contracts
contracts.text: Critical operations, constraints and success predicates are attached to the graph: service journeys, corridor operations, reaction routes or biological pathways.

stress.title: Stress-test disruption scenarios
stress.text: Sheaft simulates failures, missing observations, degraded components, demand spikes or perturbations without breaking the real system.

verdict.title: Produce a resilience verdict
verdict.text: The result is a graph-level report: fragile components, affected operations, blast radius, posture trend and recommended validation targets.

useCases.title: Three high-value domains. One resilience engine.

sre.title: SRE and digital infrastructure
sre.text: Sheaft evaluates resilience of software systems before release and between releases. It builds service graphs from trace data or topology artifacts, simulates dependency failures, and returns a gate or posture verdict for critical user journeys.
sre.support: Digital infrastructure is graph-structured operational infrastructure. Services, APIs, queues, databases, replicas, release policies and user operations form one dependency graph.

mobility.title: Smart mobility and traffic corridors
mobility.text: Sheaft evaluates resilience of smart mobility infrastructure represented as a live attributed graph: road segments, intersections, toll gates, sensors, signal controllers, payment systems and operations centers.
mobility.support: Smart mobility is cyber-physical infrastructure. Road networks, tolling, sensors and digital services form one distributed operational graph.

science.title: Chemistry and biology networks
science.text: Sheaft analyzes chemical reaction networks, biological interaction networks, metabolic pathways and bioprocess graphs to find fragile routes, critical intermediates, observation gaps and perturbation-sensitive modules.
science.support: These systems are routinely modeled as graphs: reactions connect reactants, intermediates and products; biological networks connect proteins, genes, metabolites and signaling interactions.

products.title: Two layers: model discovery and resilience assessment

bering.title: Bering: system-graph discovery and artifact publishing
bering.summary: Bering builds typed graph artifacts from telemetry, topology inputs, traces, event streams or explicit domain models. It publishes stable model and snapshot artifacts for downstream resilience analysis.
points: Discovers typed graph structure from traces, OTLP, event streams or explicit topology.
Normalizes nodes, edges, attributes and domain contracts.
Keeps provenance so teams can inspect where each part of the model came from.

sheaft.title: Sheaft: resilience simulation, verdicts and posture history
sheaft.summary: Sheaft consumes graph artifacts, stress-tests disruption scenarios, evaluates domain policies and tracks resilience posture over time.
points: Runs simulation-based resilience analysis on graph artifacts.
Evaluates policy rules for critical operations.
Reports fragile components, affected operations and blast radius.
Supports batch checks and continuous posture tracking.

proof.title: Validated on distributed systems, designed for broader networked infrastructure

icse.title: ICSE 2026 Distinguished Paper Award
icse.text: Model Discovery and Graph Simulation was recognized in ICSE 2026 NIER with a Distinguished Paper Award.

deathstar.title: DeathStarBench experiments
deathstar.text: Graph-discovered resilience models were evaluated against live fault-injection outcomes on a distributed benchmark.

otel.title: OpenTelemetry Demo experiments
otel.text: The model-discovery and simulation pipeline was tested on the OpenTelemetry Demo, including trace-derived dependencies and endpoint-level success predicates.

mvp.title: Public MVP
mvp.text: Bering and Sheaft are available as public tools for graph discovery, simulation, verdicts and posture monitoring.

takeaway.title: What this proves
takeaway.text: Sheaft can turn passive telemetry into an explicit model and produce useful resilience signals without running broad live experiments every time.

demo.title: Demo report: digital infrastructure use case
demo.text: The current public report shows the most mature software-infrastructure workflow. The same report pattern extends to mobility and scientific-network pilots.

mobility.title: Smart mobility report template
mobility.text: Corridor graph, missing observations, affected mobility journeys, disruption propagation.

science.title: Chemistry/biology report template
science.text: Reaction/pathway graph, critical intermediates, perturbation-sensitive modules, alternative routes.

pilot.title: Start with one graph, one critical operation, one historical period

sre.title: SRE pilot
sre.text: Start from trace data, topology and incident history for one service domain. Sheaft builds a model, runs failure scenarios, and compares the result with known incidents or release risks.

mobility.title: Smart mobility pilot
mobility.text: Use topology and telemetry from one corridor, tolling domain, parking/payment flow or sensorized mobility area. Sheaft identifies fragile components, missing observations and affected mobility journeys.

science.title: Chemistry/biology research pilot
science.text: Use a reaction network, biological interaction graph or pathway model with perturbation scenarios. Sheaft identifies critical intermediates, fragile modules and robustness hypotheses.

research.title: Research base: graph models, resilience and emergence
research.text: Sheaft is grounded in model discovery, graph simulation, sheaf-theoretic consistency, causal emergence and resilience monitoring. The product packages these ideas into practical workflows for networked systems.

links.asyncSemantics: Evaluating Asynchronous Semantics in Trace-Discovered Resilience Models
